Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 085 [Šurpu 4][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...]

(o 1) [...] ... [...] the Asakku-demon.

o 22

[...] x RI x [...]

o 33

[...] A₂.ZAG₂ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 3) [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 44

[... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 4) [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 55

[... ib]-ri-⸢šu a-la⸣-[ku ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 5) To go [...] of one's friend [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 66

[...] ⸢DINGIR⸣-šu la₃ ⸢ZUu⸣ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 6) [...] of one's god unwittingly, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 77

[...] mi-iḫ-⸢ru₃?⸣ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 7) [...], adversity, [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 88

[ti]-ri-iṣ-ṣi ŠU.SI mi-ḫir-[ti ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 8) The pointing of the finger, complaint against [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 99

⸢ki⸣-i na-ḫi-iš qa-bu-⸢u⸣ la ⸢šu⸣-[šu-ru BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 9) To say "how thriving," but not to make successful, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1010

⸢DINGIR⸣ u₃ diš-tar kam-lu ana [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 10) [...] the irate god and goddess [...] to [...], [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1111

⸢DINGIR⸣ šab-su diš-tar ⸢ze-ni⸣-[tu₂ ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 11) [...] the displeased god, the angry goddess, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1212

⸢ki⸣-ṣir lib₃-bi ⸢DINGIR-MEŠ⸣ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 12) [...] the god's [...] anger, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1313

⸢ar₂⸣-nu ⸢šu⸣-[su-ḫu ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 13) To remove the transgression, [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1414

[...] ... [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 14) [...] ... [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1515

[...] ŠU-MIN ⸢en-ši ṣa⸣-[ba-tu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 15) [...], to hold the weak one by the hand, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1616

[šim]-ti ⸢ḪULti₃ šu-pe⸣-[lu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 16) To overturn an evil fate, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1717

[MAŠ₂].⸢GE₆ ḪUL⸣[ti₃] du-um-mu-[qu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 17) To improve an evil dream, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1818

[d]⸢LAMMA SIG₅ a-na⸣ [...] x-šu₂ ša₂-ra-ku [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 18) To bestow a good tutelary goddess upon [...], ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1919

[...] ⸢MAŠ₂.GE₆-MEŠ A₂-MEŠ GISKIM⸣-MEŠ a-na NA la₃ ⸢TEe⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 19) To avert from a man [...] of dreams, ominous conditions (and) signs, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 2020

[...] uk-ku-šu ⸢munusPEŠ₄⸣ [qa₂]-⸢du⸣ ša₂ ⸢ŠA₃-ša₂ šul-lu⸣-[mu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 20) To expel [...], to keep the pregant woman together with her unborn child safe, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 2121

[šu]-⸢lu⸣-ud-du šu-⸢mu⸣ šur-⸢šu⸣-[u BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 21) To cause to give birth (and) to provide a son, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2222

[...] x ša₂-⸢su-u₂⸣ EN ḫa-sa-[su BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 22) To shout [...] ..., to remember the lord, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2323

[...] ⸢re⸣-e-ma le-qu-u₂ un-[nin-ni BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 23) [...] mercy, to accept supplications, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2424

[... kun]-nu-u niš DINGIR-MEŠ za-⸢ka⸣-[ru BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 24) To treat kindly [...], to swear on the life of the gods, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2525

[... ma-li]-⸢tu₂⸣ ina ID₂⸢i⸣ x [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 25) ... [...] a full [...] in the river, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2626

[... i]-ša₂-riš [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 26) [...] correctly [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

[...] x x x [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

[šal]-⸢lu⸣ u ⸢ka⸣-[ma-a ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 31) [...] the captive and the bound one [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3232

a-na IGI UN-⸢MEŠ⸣-[šu₂ ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 32) [...] in the presence of his people, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3333

ina šer₃-te e-ṭe-ru [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 33) To save from guilt, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3434

ina GIG ⸢šu⸣-<ut>-⸢bu⸣-u [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 34) To raise from illness, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3535

ina ar-⸢ni⸣ ZI? ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 35) To remove from transgression, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3636

⸢ina⸣ ḫa-aš-ti šu-lu-u ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 36) To raise from a hole, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3737

[ša₂] ⸢GIDIM?⸣ lem-nu iṣ-ba-⸢tu-šu⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 37) [Whom] an evil ghost has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3838

[ša₂] ⸢UDUG?⸣ lem-nu iṣ-ba-⸢tu-šu⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 38) [Whom] an evil utukku-demon has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 3939

[ša₂] ⸢DINGIR⸣ lem-nu iṣ-ba-⸢tu⸣-šu [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 39) [Whom] an evil god has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4040

[...] ... [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma ... iṣ]-ba-⸢tu-šu⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 40) [...] ... [... ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe); ...] has seized [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4141

[...] BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma :: ša₂ d⸢DIM₉.ME.LAGAB?⸣ iṣ-ba-tu-šu ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 41) [...] ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe) :: Whom an ahhāzu-demon has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4242

[...] iṣ-ba-tu-šu ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 42) [...] has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4343

[...] ⸢niš⸣ DINGIR šib-sat DINGIR me-ḫer-te DINGIR ta-⸢zi-im⸣-tu₂ ma-mit

(o 43) [...], the life of the god, the anger of the god, the complaint against the god, lament, oath, [...], elder brother (and) elder sister, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4444

[...] ⸢ŠEŠ⸣ GALu NIN GALte <<DU>> BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

o 4545

[...] ⸢lip⸣-šur-ru DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 45) May the great gods release (all these) [...], ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4646

[1en lip]-⸢šur⸣ dUTU qu-⸢ra⸣-du BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 46) [First, may] Šamaš, the warrior, release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4747

[2u₂ lip]-⸢šur⸣ d30 dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 47) [Second, may] Sin (and) Nergal release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4848

[3 lip]-⸢šur⸣ d15 dPAP.SUKKAL da-nu-ni-tu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 48) [3, may] Ištar, Papsukkal (and) Annunitu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4949

[4 lip]-šur da-nu₃ u dEN.LIL₂!(KI) de₂-a dNIN.⸢MAḪ⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 49) [4, may] Anu and Ellil, Ea (and) Ninmah release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5050

[5 lip]-šur dIŠKUR dMAŠ dza-ba₄-ba₄ d⸢TIŠPAK⸣ [d]⸢NIN⸣.GIR₂.SU BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 50) [5, may] Adad, Ninurta, Zababa, Tišpak (and) Ningirsu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5151

[6 lip]-šur dURAŠ dMES dASAR.⸢RE⸣ [dASAR].LU₂.ḪI dAN.GAL dtu-⸢tu⸣

(o 51) [6, may] Uraš, Marduk, Asare, Asalluhu, Angal, (and) Tutu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

Reverse
r 1r 1

[7 lip-šur d7].BI DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ šu-tu₂-ḫa-⸢a⸣ [... lip]-ṭur BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma1

(r 1) [7, may] the Seven, great gods, [release]; may the extended gods absolve [...], ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 22

[... li]-⸢sap⸣-pi-ḫu!(RI) ki-ṣir lum-ni ⸢li-par-ri⸣-[ru] ⸢ka⸣-si-tu₂ li-ra-mu-u ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 2) [May] they disperse [...]; may they dissolve the knot of evil; may they release the magical constraint, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 33

[...] niš DINGIR lip-su-su ar₂-nam <<li-x>> li-⸢is⸣-su-ḫu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 3) [...]; may they cancel (the oath on) the life of the god; may they remove the transgression, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 44

[...] ⸢lis⸣-su-u ḫi-ṭi-tu₂ li-šal-li-mu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 4) May they eradicate [...]; may they correct the mistake, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 55

[... lib]-⸢luṭ⸣ ma-aq-tu₂ lit-bi ka-su-u ⸢li-šir⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 5) [May ...] recover; may the fallen one stand up; may the captive become right, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 66

[... li-in]-⸢ṭa-šir⸣ ša₂ E₂ ⸢ṣi-bit⸣-te ZALAG₂ ⸢li⸣-mur BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 6) [May ...] be released; may the prisoner see the light, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 77

[... KI]-⸢šu₂⸣ šab-su ina ⸢u₄-me⸣ an-ne₂-e ⸢li⸣-is-⸢li-mu KI⸣-šu₂ ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 7) May [...], who are angry [with] him, be reconciled with him on this day, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 88

[...] ⸢diš₈?⸣-tar₂-šu₂ ša₂ NENNI A NENNI ⸢li-paṭ-ṭir-aš₂-šu₂⸣ li-pa-⸢šir⸣-šu₂ ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 8) May [...] his goddess's anger with so-and-so, son of so-and-so, be dispelled from him; may he be released, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 99

[... lit-ta]-⸢bi⸣-ik ina u₄-me an-⸢ni⸣-i BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 9) May [...] be expunged on this day, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1010

[...] lu pa-⸢ṭa⸣-ra-šu₂ ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 10) [...]; may it be released from him, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1111

[...] gil₂-la-te-šu₂ [ana] A-MEŠ ŠUBu [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 11) May [...] (and) his sins be thrown [into] the water, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1212

[...] ⸢li-ma-ša₂⸣-a [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 12) May [...] be forgotten, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1313

[...] ⸢ma⸣-ma-tu-[šu₂ ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 13) [... his] curses [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1414

[... li]-tak-ki-[šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 14) [May ...] be expelled, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1515

di-iʾ-[a-šu₂] ⸢di-lip?⸣-[ta-šu₂ ni]-sa-su [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 15) [His] headache, [his] anxiety, his wailing [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1616

u₈-a-[a ...] x x [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 16) Woe, [...] ... [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1717

ina u₄-me ⸢an⸣-[ni-i ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 17) [...] on this day, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1818

a-na [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 18) At [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 1919

⸢ar⸣-[ni ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 19) The transgression [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)].

(4 lines missing)
r 2424

⸢KI.MIN⸣<(li-iz-ziz)> [...]

(r 24) [...], ditto (may he be present); [...].

r 2525

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> [...] ⸢EN⸣ [...] ⸢mur⸣-[ṣu]

(r 25) [...], lord of [...], ditto (may he be present); [...] illness.

r 2626

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> ⸢dTIŠPAK⸣ EN EREN₂ni [li]-⸢na?-kir⸣ di-[ʾu]

(r 26) Tišpak, lord of the troops, ditto (may he be present); may he eliminate a headache.

r 2727

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dMAŠ EN <giš>TUKUL li-[ni-is]-⸢si⸣ pu-uš-[qu]

(r 27) Ninurta, lord of the weapon, ditto (may he be present); may he remove hardship.

r 2828

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dPAP.SUKKAL EN <giš>GIDRU ⸢li-ri⸣-iq ⸢mur⸣-[ṣu]

(r 28) Papsukkal, lord of the sceptre, ditto (may he be present); may he keep illness at a distance.

r 2929

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dMES ABGAL DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢mu⸣SILIM ⸢ZI⸣[ti₃]

(r 29) Marduk, sage of the gods, safeguarder of life, ditto (may he be present).

r 3030

[KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz])> ⸢d⸣ASAR.LU₂.ḪI MAŠ.MAŠ DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ ša₂ ina te-šu₂ lu₂UŠ₂ x [...]

(r 30) Asalluhi, incantation priest among the great gods, upon whose incantation-formula a dead man [...], ditto (may he be present).

r 3131

[KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> d]NERGALₓ(U.GUR) EN šip-⸢ṭi⸣ [...] NAM.TAR ⸢im?⸣-[me-du ...]

(r 31) Nergal, lord of judgement, [...] (and) fate take refuge [...], ditto (may he be present).

r 3232

[...]

(r 32) [...].

r 3333

[KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dza]-⸢ba₄⸣-ba₄ ⸢EN⸣ [...]

(r 33) Zababa, lord [...], ditto (may he be present); [...].

r 3434

[KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dEN.NU].⸢GI⸣ EN ⸢E⸣ [...]

(r 34) Ennugi, lord of the dyke [...], ditto (may he be present); [...].

r 3535

[KI.MIN]<(li-iz-ziz)> d⸢NUSKA SUKKAL⸣ E₂.KUR [...]

(r 35) Nusku, minister of the Ekur, ditto (may he be present); [...].

r 3636

⸢KI.MIN⸣<(li-iz-ziz)> dGIRA₂ [...] ... [...]

(r 36) Giru, [...], ditto (may he be present); [...].

r 3737

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> diš-tar ⸢be⸣-let ⸢KUR⸣.[KUR ...]

(r 37) Ištar, lady of the lands, ditto (may she be present); [...].

r 3838

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dGU.⸢LA⸣ a-zu-gal-[la-tu ...]

(r 38) Gula, chief physician, ditto (may she be present); [...].

r 3939

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dba-⸢ba₆⸣ di-[lip-ta-šu₂ ...]

(r 39) Baba, ditto (may she be present); [... his] anxiety.

(catchline)
r 4040

EN₂ aš₂ ⸢ḫul⸣ gal₅-⸢la₂-gin₇⸣ [...]

(r 40) Incantation: A bad curse like a gallu-demon [...].

(colophon)
r 4141

⸢IM m⸣SUḪUŠ—DINGIR lu₂ŠAB.TUR DUMU [...]

(r 41) Tablet of Ubru-ili, scribal apprentice, son of [...].

1The reading of šu-tu₂-ha-a is uncertain.
